At GLG, you are an extension of our clients’ core teams. You will work at the intersection of industries and global markets, navigating high\-stakes challenges across the full spectrum of their strategic initiatives. Operating within the industry’s most trusted research environment, you’ll help our clients capture the nuanced perspectives that drive smarter, faster business outcomes. We are a global team of pragmatic problem\-solvers who mirror the intensity of the markets we serve. If you are driven by intellectual curiosity and a bias toward action, join us in reinventing the industry we invented. Gerson Lehrman Group, Inc. (“GLG”) is an equal opportunity employer and will not discriminate against any employee or applicant on the basis of age, race, religion, color, marital status, disability, gender, national origin, sexual orientation, veteran status, or any classification protected by federal, state, or local law.

title: Audit Associate
description: **Newly Qualified Accountants – Big 4 Audit Background \- Wealth \& Asset Management** Multiple Opportunities \| Dublin / Hybrid \| Excellent Salary \+ Bonus \+ Benefits Are you a **newly qualified accountant** coming from a **Big 4 Audit** background and ready to take the next step in your career? We are partnering with a range of leading multinational, financial services, and high\-growth organisations seeking top talent for exciting opportunities across industry. This is an excellent chance to move from practice into a commercial role where you can broaden your experience, gain exposure to senior stakeholders, and accelerate your long\-term career progression. Opportunities Available: * Financial Accountant * Finance Business Partner * Internal Audit * FP\&A Analyst * Regulatory Reporting * Fund / Financial Services Accounting What We’re Looking For: * ACA / ACCA / CPA newly qualified or due to qualify soon * Trained within a **Big 4 / Top 10 Audit firm** * Strong technical accounting knowledge * Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills * Ambitious, commercial mindset with desire to progress What’s On Offer: * Highly competitive salary packages * Bonus \+ pension \+ healthcare * Hybrid and flexible working options * Clear progression pathways * Opportunity to move into industry with top\-tier employers If you are ready to leverage your Big 4 training into a high\-impact role, we’d love to speak with you in confidence

title: Banking Associate
description: This is an exciting opportunity to join a globally recognized, top\-tier international law firm with a reputation for excellence in banking and finance. The firm is known for its innovative approach, high\-profile client base, and collaborative culture. With offices spanning key financial hubs worldwide, the firm offers exposure to complex cross\-border transactions and cutting\-edge legal work. **Role Overview** We are seeking a talented Banking Associate with 2 years of post\-qualification experience (PQE) to join a market\-leading Banking \& Finance team. This role offers the chance to work on sophisticated financing transactions for some of the world’s most prominent financial institutions, corporations, and private equity sponsors. **Key Responsibilities** * Advising clients on a wide range of banking and finance matters, including corporate lending, leveraged finance, acquisition finance, real estate finance, and restructuring. * Drafting, reviewing, and negotiating loan agreements, security documents, agreements, and other related documentation. * Supporting clients on cross\-border transactions by collaborating with colleagues across the firm's global network. * Providing strategic advice on regulatory compliance and risk management in relation to banking and finance matters. * Managing multiple transactions simultaneously while maintaining high\-quality standards under tight deadlines. * Building strong relationships with clients by understanding their commercial objectives and delivering tailored solutions. **Requirements** * Qualified lawyer with 2 years of post\-qualification experience (PQE) in banking and finance law from a leading law firm or reputable in\-house legal team. * Strong academic background with a degree in Law (LLB or equivalent). * Proven experience working on complex financing transactions; cross\-border experience is highly desirable. * Excellent drafting skills with strong attention to detail. * Commercially astute with the ability to provide practical legal advice that aligns with clients’ business goals. * Strong interpersonal skills with the ability to build rapport with clients and colleagues at all levels. * A proactive mindset with a commitment to delivering exceptional client service in a fast\-paced environment. **What We Offer** * The opportunity to work at one of the world’s most prestigious law firms alongside industry\-leading professionals. * Exposure to high\-value, complex transactions for blue\-chip clients across multiple jurisdictions. * A supportive and inclusive culture that values diversity and fosters professional growth. * Access to world\-class training programs designed to enhance your technical expertise and leadership capabilities. * Competitive remuneration package and benefits tailored to attract top talent in the market. Robert Walters Operations Limited is an employment business and employment agency and welcomes applications from all candidates

title: Barista
description: **Barista Join Our Team and Brew Up a Great Career with Us!** **The Role:** This isnt just another job its your chance to **kickstart a career** in a brand that values **growth, development, and promoting from within.** As a Barista, youll be part of a supportive team where learning is part of every day and your potential truly matters. **Why Join Us?** Full training provided **no experience needed!** A clear path to progress many of our leaders started as Baristas Ongoing support to help you grow in confidence and skills A fun, energetic environment where every shift brings something new **What We Offer:** Complimentary handcrafted coffee on every shift Employee discounts across all Costa locations Full training and ongoing development opportunities A friendly, inclusive, and supportive team culture A workplace where you can grow, learn, and be yourself **Make it Yours:** This role is based in **Dundrum Town Centre\- vibrant shopping district!** **Apply today and bring your love for coffee to life with Costa!**

title: Barista
description: **Barista at Caffè Nero ROI** ----------------------------- At Caffè Nero, it’s our teams that make us special. That’s why we’re looking for warm, positive people who love working with others to come and join us. If you want to be part of a fantastic team, with endless opportunities to learn and grow, this could be the perfect next step for you! **What you can expect:** * Brilliant training – no experience is required! * The opportunity to grow your career and develop personally and professionally. * Via our incentive ‘Nero 90’, our Baristas and Shift Leaders also have the opportunity to receive an additional 15c per hour, if their store collectively achieves the Customer Satisfaction score. * Paid breaks. * Unlimited free handmade drinks and up to 75% off food and packaged drinks when you’re on shift. * 50% off all handmade drinks and up to 40% off everything else when you’re off shift. * Additional pay on Bank Holidays. * Recommend a Friend scheme – a cash reward if you find new people for the team! * Access to our benefits platform, with hundreds of discounts, rewards and offers. **Why choose us?** When you join us, you’re joining a group of caring, supportive and friendly people – whether that’s your team, the regular customers of your café or your community. At the same time as giving fantastic service, you’ll have loads of fun along the way, whether that’s a pizza night, a Caffè Nero Club party or even at the Barista of the Year event. There’s something for everyone. Our Baristas earn €14\.15 per hour in this location. If you have a great attitude, bags of energy and love interacting with customers then we’d love to hear from you! **At Caffè Nero you can really be you, with us.** ROICN2021

title: Business Processes Associate Consultant
description: **We help the world run better** At SAP, we keep it simple: you bring your best to us, and we'll bring out the best in you. We're builders touching over 20 industries and 80% of global commerce, and we need your unique talents to help shape what's next. The work is challenging – but it matters. You'll find a place where you can be yourself, prioritize your wellbeing, and truly belong. What's in it for you? Constant learning, skill growth, great benefits, and a team that wants you to grow and succeed. **What you bring** * 0 \- 3 years of experience in business process consulting and service delivery * Strong knowledge of industry and end‑to‑end SAP Intelligent Enterprise processes * Experience in data management, feasibility studies, and complex customer engagements * Project or program management expertise (PMI, Six Sigma, or equivalent preferred) * Strong communication skills and emotional intelligence * University degree or equivalent experience; SAP solution certifications preferred * Fluent English required; additional languages a plus **What you’ll build** * Translate customer vision into end‑to‑end business process and data management solutions * Evaluate industry and technology trends to shape innovative process and solution roadmaps * Design, map, and optimize business processes aligned to enterprise IT architectures * Lead solution design across SAP and non‑SAP landscapes (on‑premise, cloud, hybrid) * Drive feasibility studies, solution reviews, and innovation testing initiatives * Represent SAP with customers, delivering high‑quality outcomes on complex, high‑impact engagements * Support presales activities, customer workshops, and executive‑level discussions * Deliver and implement best‑practice business processes across LoBs and global rollouts * Drive change management and support transitions to S/4HANA and cloud solutions * Manage or support complex projects, ensuring milestones, quality, and budgets are met * Contribute to the evolution of SAP’s services portfolio, templates, and delivery assets * Act as (sub‑)project lead or team lead when required, using SAP Activate methodologies * Mentor and coach business process consultants, supporting people development and knowledge sharing * Continuously develop your expertise through certifications, innovation initiatives, and community leadership **Where you belong** At SAP, you will belong to a diverse and inclusive community that values your contributions and supports your growth. At Global Delivery Hub EMEA, our mission is to lead our customers to success on their business transformation journey to the Cloud. You will have the opportunity to work on critical customer projects, collaborate with experts across SAP, and make a meaningful impact on the success of our customers. Join us to bring your skills, build innovative solutions, and belong to a team that drives transformation and success. **Bring out your best** SAP innovations help more than four hundred thousand customers worldwide work together more efficiently and use business insight more effectively. Originally known for leadership in enterprise resource planning (ERP) software, SAP has evolved to become a market leader in end\-to\-end business application software and related services for database, analytics, intelligent technologies, and experience management. As a cloud company with two hundred million users and more than one hundred thousand employees worldwide, we are purpose\-driven and future\-focused, with a highly collaborative team ethic and commitment to personal development. Whether connecting global industries, people, or platforms, we help ensure every challenge gets the solution it deserves. At SAP, you can bring out your best. **We win with inclusion** SAP’s culture of inclusion, focus on health and well\-being, and flexible working models help ensure that everyone – regardless of background – feels included and can run at their best. At SAP, we believe we are made stronger by the unique capabilities and qualities that each person brings to our company, and we invest in our employees to inspire confidence and help everyone realize their full potential. We ultimately believe in unleashing all talent and creating a better world. SAP is committed to the values of Equal Employment Opportunity and provides accessibility accommodations to applicants with physical and/or mental disabilities. If you are interested in applying for employment with SAP and are in need of accommodation or special assistance to navigate our website or to complete your application, please send an e\-mail with your request to Recruiting Operations Team: Careers@sap.com. For SAP employees: Only permanent roles are eligible for the SAP Employee Referral Program, according to the eligibility rules set in the SAP Referral Policy. Specific conditions may apply for roles in Vocational Training. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to their age, race, religion, national origin, ethnicity, gender (including pregnancy, childbirth, et al), sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, protected veteran status, or disability, in compliance with applicable federal, state, and local legal requirements. Successful candidates might be required to undergo a background verification with an external vendor. **AI Usage in the Recruitment Process** For information on the responsible use of AI in our recruitment process, please refer to our Guidelines for Ethical Usage of AI in the Recruiting Process. Please note that any violation of these guidelines may result in disqualification from the hiring process. Requisition ID: 452289 \| Work Area: Consulting and Professional Services \| Expected Travel: 0% \| Career Status: Graduate \| Employment Type: Regular Full Time \| Additional Locations: \#LI\-Hybrid

title: Business and Operations Apprenticeships in the Civil Service
description: **Job Title: Business and Operations Apprenticeships** **Employer:** Civil Service **Location:** Donegal, Dublin, Galway, Kildare, Laois, Leitrim **Closing Date:** 3pm, Thursday, 30 April 2026 **Salary:** Starting at €28,814 As a Business and Operations Apprentice, you will earn as you learn and gain a recognised qualification alongside practical hands\-on experience working for one of a variety of Civil Service organisations. Whether you are school leaver, starting out in your career, looking to upskill, or change career, this apprenticeship may be for you. The comprehensive teaching and learning experience will lay a strong foundation for long\-term career growth in the Civil Service. As a Business and Operations Apprentice, you will improve your skills and knowledge in areas such as: * improving operational delivery and business performance * budgeting and financial management * providing quality customer service to the public * project management and evaluating performance * the use of technology and data to improve processes * human resources and talent management. Throughout the programme, you will also build your understanding of how public services are delivered, engage with a variety of business functions, and improve your digital and leadership capabilities in a changing world of work. For the English language stream, positions are available in Dublin, Kildare, Laois and Leitrim. Some of the positions available in this competition will provide an opportunity for suitable candidates to develop their Irish skills further, through an Irish language stream in the following locations: Donegal (Gaoth Dobhair) and Galway (Na Forbacha). Candidates who successfully complete their apprenticeship will be given the opportunity to continue to develop their career at Executive Officer level, which is the entry grade to junior management in the Civil Service. A full list of essential requirements is available in the Information Booklet. The closing date for receipt of completed applications is 3pm on Thursday, 30 April, 2026\. We are committed to a policy of equal opportunity and encourage applications under all nine grounds of the Employment Equality Act.

title: Butcher
description: **Job Ref** 51924 **Job Title** Butcher **Job Type** Full time Location: Johnstownbridge, Enfield, Co Meath **Salary** On Application **Closing Date For Applications** 27\-04\-2026 **Job Description** **Main purpose of the role:** Ensure the Meat Department operates efficiently and effectively at all times and provide our customers with excellent quality and products and services. **The Ideal Candidate Will Have/be** * Craft butchery course is desirable * Numerical skills * Excellent communication skills * Previous customer service experience is an advantage * The ability to work as part of a team in a fast\-paced environment, ability to multi task under pressure * A passion for food and the ability to inspire shoppers * HACCP training is beneficial but not essential. **Main duties:** * Actively live Centra brand\-values i.e. Proud, Energetic, Imaginative and Community\-Based * Maintain retail cuts at a high standard in accordance with customer needs * Drive sales through ensuring SOPs and store standards are met on a daily basis * Merchandise and present department to the highest standard * Implement planograms correctly * Control stock rotation so that quality and freshness of goods is of the highest standard * Manage waste and shrink in the department * Ensure continuous improvement in the Meat Department by remaining up to date with new products and initiatives * Enhance product knowledge by gaining information from a range of sources and share ideas, suggestions and builds * Engage with new initiatives and embrace new ways of working. **About The Locality** O\`Neill\`s Centra is a family run store in the village of Johnstownbridge, outside Enfield, in Co Meath. We are now seeking an experienced Senior Butcher / Qualified Butcher to join our team and play a key role in the continued growth of the business. Key Responsibilities Retail butchery, meat cutting, boning, trimming, and portion control Preparation and presentation of fresh Irish meat to the highest standards Ensure full compliance with food safety, HACCP, and hygiene standards Support management across a two\-shop operation, maintaining consistency and quality Lead and support team members on the shop floor Contribute to product development, value\-added meat products, and seasonal ranges Deliver excellent customer service in a retail food environment The Ideal Candidate 3\-5\+ years\` experience as a Senior Butcher / Qualified Butcher / Meat Cutter Strong knowledge of traditional butchery and Irish meat provenance Experience in a retail butcher or food service environment Interest in innovation and modern food trends Reliable, professional, and quality\-focused Ability to work as part of a team and support junior staff What We Offer €40,000\- €50,000 (negotiable, depending on experience) Full\-time position 20 days annual leave No Sunday work No Bank Holiday Mondays Please note that the start and finish times stated are only an approximate Apply

title: Candidate Clinical Nurse Specialist (cCNS) in Occupational Health
description: **Role Title:** Candidate Clinical Nurse Specialist (cCNS) in Occupational Health **Job Reference Number:** 002289 **Closing Date:** Monday, 4th May 2026 **Purpose of the Role:** The purpose of this role is to deliver specialist nursing care in line with the five core concepts set out in the Framework for the Establishment of Clinical Nurse Specialist posts (4th edition), National Council for the Professional Development of Nursing and Midwifery (NCNM), 2008\. This role has been developed to support a change for specialist practice to develop and meet service need and in line with National policy as set out by “A Policy on the Development of Graduate to Advanced Nursing and Midwifery Practice” (DoH, 2019\). The successful candidate will work as a member of the multidisciplinary team providing a patient centered quality and seamless service. **Details of the service/Background to the post** The post holder will be required to work as a member of the multidisciplinary team in providing and developing an efficient and effective Occupational Health service for all Hospital personnel. Responsibility for excellent communication and the co\-ordination of service delivery rests with the Occupational Health nurse. The responsibility of the post holder entails not alone managing and enhancing existing services but identifying and implementing new initiatives to optimise staff wellbeing and an overall quality service. **Required:** * Be a registered nurse/midwife on the active Register of Nurses and Midwives held by An Bord Altranais and Cnáimhseachais na hÉireann (Nursing and Midwifery Board of Ireland) or be eligible to be so registered. **AND** * Be registered in the division(s) of the Nursing and Midwifery Board of Ireland (Bord Altranais agus Cnáimhseachais na hÉireann) Register for which the application is being made or be entitled to be so registered. **AND** * Have a minimum of 1 years’ post registration full time experience or an aggregate of 1 years’ full time experience in the division of the register in which the application is being made. **AND** * Have a minimum of 1 years’ experience or an aggregate of 1 years’ full time experience in specialist area of Occupational Health care within the last 3 years. **AND** * Have successfully completed a post registration programme of study, as certified by the education provider which verifies that the applicant has achieved a Quality and Qualifications Ireland (QQI), National Framework of Qualifications (NFQ) major academic Level 9 or higher award (equivalent to 60 ECTS or above) that is relevant to the specialist area of care and in line with the requirements for specialist practice as set out by the National Council for Nursing and Midwifery 4th ed. (2008\). **OR** Alternatively provide written evidence from the Higher Education Institute that they have achieved the number of ECTS credits equivalent to a Level 9 or higher standard (60 ECTS or above), relevant to the specialist area of care and in line with the requirements for specialist practice as set out by the National Council for Nursing and Midwifery 4th ed. (2008\) Occupational Health of care prior to application (\*\*See note below). **OR** If the applicant does not possess the relevant QQI NFQ Level 9 qualification, the applicant will be supported to undertake the required postgraduate education, at QQI NFQ level 9 qualification (equivalent to 60 ECTS or above) relevant to the specialist area. **AND** * Be required to demonstrate that they have continuing professional development (CPD) relevant to the specialist area or will be supported to obtain the required CPD. **AND** * Have the ability to practice safely and effectively fulfilling their professional responsibility within their scope of practice. **AND** * Candidates must possess the requisite knowledge and ability, including a high standard of suitability and clinical, professional, and administrative capacity for the proper discharge of the duties of the office. \*\*Note: For Nurses/Midwives who express an interest in CNS/CMS roles and who currently hold a level 8 educational qualification in the specialist area (equivalent to 60 ECTS or above), this qualification will be recognised up to September 2026\. The clinical experience requirements for this cohort of nurses/midwives remain consistent with the DoH (2019\) policy, i.e. a minimum of 1 years’ experience in practice and a minimum of 1 years’ experience in the specialist area and they could be supported to progress on a candidate CNS/CMS Pathway. All of the above must be achieved within 2 years for this pathway. The Candidate Clinical Nurse Specialist is required to progress to the Registered Clinical Nurse Specialist grade within 2 years of commencement of this pathway and formally apply for entry onto the Interim ONMSD CNS/CMS database (until the database is transferred to its permanent location). * Demonstrate I.T. skills including excellent keyboard skills. * Experience in teaching and assessing the Nursing and Health Care Assistant team. * Experience in carrying out audits. **Desired:** * Nurse Prescribing Registration \- or agree to undertake within an agreed timeframe, the nurse prescribing of Medicinal Products Certificate. * Have undertaken or agree to undertake, within an agreed timeframe, the Nurse Prescribing of Ionising Radiation Certificate. * Working knowledge of healthcare patient record systems.

title: Carpentry & Joinery Apprentice
description: **Overview** At Sisk, we create places and infrastructure that shape how people live, work and connect – now and for future generations. As a family‑owned, international construction and engineering group, we offer meaningful careers across building, infrastructure, rail, energy and off‑site manufacturing throughout Ireland, the UK and Europe, where people are supported to grow, make an impact and be part of positive change. Exciting opportunity alert! Join Sisk for a rewarding Carpentry \& Joinery Apprenticeship in Dublin. 🛠️ 👉 4\-year program blending hands\-on experience at our joinery center with on\-site projects. 👉 7 comprehensive phases, including training at ETB or Institute of Technology. 👉 Culminates in a prestigious QQI Level 6 Advanced Craft Certificate. Ready to kickstart your construction career? **Qualifications** * Leaving Cert / Leaving Cert Applied \- must have + Construction Studies preferred but not mandatory **Additional Information** Our people are at the heart of our success and our proposition to them is work with purpose, have time for life, build a career with no limits and surround yourself with great people. Our Internal Recruitment Team fulfil all vacancies on a direct basis. Unsolicited contact by unapproved agencies (sharing speculative CVs) will not be accepted and introductory fees will not apply. At Sisk we are committed to attracting and hiring diverse talent into our organisation. We know the confidence gap and imposter phenomenon can get in the way of meeting remarkable candidates. We also know that candidates from underrepresented backgrounds often don't apply for roles if they don't feel they meet all the criteria. Give your transferable skills the chance to tell the full story. It’s not all about your technical expertise. Don’t hold back, we’d love to hear from you.

title: Change Analyst Graduate
description: Change Analyst Graduate, Ballycoolin Business Park, Dublin Apply Now **Mercury is the European leader in construction solutions.** We build and manage complex engineering \& construction projects for the world’s leading corporations. Our solutions help deliver technologies and life changing advancements that connect people, communities and businesses, giving them the power to achieve incredible things. Our people have the courage to be innovative. Their determination and sharp focus enable us to deliver with certainty, time and time again. We deliver our clients’ visions through leading edge construction solutions across multiple sectors, including Enterprise Data Centres, Advanced Technology \& Life Sciences, Hyperscale Data Centres and Fire Protection. At Mercury, it is our duty to encourage and back our people to realise their vision of themselves. We place them at the heart of what we do, providing challenging opportunities to develop within a great team in a supportive environment that allows them to reach their full potential. **Our Graduate Programme** We’re seeking the leaders of tomorrow to join us on our Graduate Programme \- those who are willing to share our values, live our culture and deliver beyond expectations. At Mercury we know that to achieve incredible things you must invest in your people. Our dedication to shaping the leaders of tomorrow shines through our highly regarded graduate programme and ensures we stay ahead in a competitive market. From day one you will be on a stellar career trajectory with no limits to what you can achieve, and the programme empowers you to develop the essential skills needed to excel in the fast\-paced construction industry. Since launching in 2009, we have successfully trained 360\+ graduates, many of whom have forged long\-lasting careers with us. Our comprehensive eighteen\-month programme is tailored to meet real\-world demands, featuring courses led by both external and internal subject matter experts in a focused learning environment. Graduates on our programme can anticipate working alongside a diverse and supportive team, accessing state\-of\-the\-art training and masterclasses, and gaining international experience at the leading edge of industry. An important aspect of the development programme is the completion of a group research project. Graduates are split into small groups of four or five and assigned a strategic business project to research and present their findings to Senior Management. All topics are chosen by Senior Management and are relevant to Mercury's business **Role Purpose** An entry\-level opportunity has arisen for a graduate to join our change management team. The Change Analyst role is ideal for an individual seeking experience in transformation, change management and business operations. The Change Analyst provides analytical, coordination and documentation support across Mercury’s change initiatives. This role helps assess impacts, prepare readiness information, produce governance materials, and support the rollout of Mercury’s Change Framework. **Rols \& Responsibilities** * Business Readiness Support + Support the Change Lead in coordinating readiness activities across teams. + Assist in identifying impacts to people, processes and systems. + Help prepare readiness checklists, stakeholder maps, and cutover support materials. + Gather information from functions to track readiness status, risks and dependencies. * Change Framework \& Documentation + Use and maintain Change Framework templates + Support quality checks on documentation before submission to the CCC. + Help update templates and guidance materials based on lessons learned and feedback. + Maintain SharePoint folders, version control and documentation standards. * Communications \& Stakeholder Engagement + Draft communications, updates and briefing materials for impacted teams. + Support workshop preparation and facilitation (slides, notes, logistics). + Help track stakeholder questions, concerns and follow\-up actions. * Change Control \& Governance Support + Assist with data collection for impact scoring, risk profiles, and dependencies. + Track mitigation plans, decision conditions and follow\-up actions. + Maintain the change decision log and ensure it is kept up to date. * Portfolio \& Reporting Support + Assist PMO with data inputs for portfolio dashboards and reporting. + Track small\-scale changes and help identify collisions or overlaps. + Maintain trackers for readiness, risks, actions and adoption progress. * Continuous Improvement + Capture lessons learned and contribute to process improvements. + Suggest enhancements to templates, tools or ways of working. + Take part in developing Mercury’s maturing Change Function. + Conduct trend analysis on change initiatives approved and rejected **Essential Criteria** * Graduate degree (Business, HR, Communications, Engineering, Data, or related field). * Strong written and verbal communication skills. * Structured thinker who can bring order and consistency to information * Comfortable coordinating with multiple stakeholders. * Proficiency in Microsoft Office and collaboration tools (e.g., SharePoint, Teams). * Ability to organise work and manage multiple tasks. * Interest in change management, transformation, PMO or business analysis. **Desirable Criteria** * Experience in the Construction Industry or Trade Background ***Mercury is an equal opportunities employer.*** Apply Now

title: Chef de Partie
description: **Our Mission** We’re passionate about creating unforgettable dining experiences that delight our guests and celebrate the artistry of food. Every plate we serve tells a story—crafted with precision, creativity, and care. Our kitchen thrives on teamwork, respect, and a shared commitment to excellence, ensuring that every service is as memorable as the last. **The Impact You'll Make** As a Chef de Partie, you’ll be the guardian of your station, ensuring every dish that leaves it meets our exacting standards for taste, presentation, and consistency. You’ll mentor junior chefs, collaborate with the wider kitchen team, and bring your own flair to the menu—helping us deliver dishes that keep guests coming back. **What You'll Do** * Manage and run your assigned kitchen section (e.g., grill, pastry, sauce) with precision and efficiency. * Prepare, cook, and present dishes to the highest standard, following recipes and plating guidelines. * Train and supervise Commis Chefs, fostering their growth and confidence. * Maintain impeccable hygiene and safety standards, ensuring compliance with food safety regulations. * Work closely with the Head Chef and Sous Chef to develop new menu items and seasonal specials. * Monitor stock levels, minimize waste, and assist with ordering supplies. **What You'll Bring** * Proven experience as a Chef de Partie or strong experience as a Demi Chef ready to step up. * Expertise in your station’s techniques, with a passion for continuous learning. * Strong organizational skills and the ability to work calmly under pressure. * A collaborative spirit and a commitment to supporting your team. * Knowledge of food safety and hygiene best practices. **Why You'll Love It Here** * A supportive, creative kitchen environment where your ideas are valued. * Opportunities for professional development, including culinary training and cross\-station experience. * Staff meals provided during shifts. * Competitive salary and tips share. * Discounts on dining across our restaurant group. * A team culture built on respect, passion, and pride in our craft. Pay: €30,000\.00\-€35,000\.00 per year Work Location: In person

title: Church Musicians ( Drums, bass guitar, Piano/organ)
description: Faith City Church is seeking committed and skilled musicians to join our vibrant choir and worship team. We are currently looking for individuals who play **drums, bass guitar, and piano/organ** to support our weekly services and rehearsals. This role is ideal for musicians who are not only technically proficient but also have a strong personal relationship with Christ and a heart for worship ministry. **Key Responsibilities:** * Participate in weekly rehearsals and Sunday services * Collaborate with choir members and worship leaders * Contribute to a spiritually uplifting worship experience * Maintain consistency and commitment to scheduled sessions **Requirements:** * Proficiency in one of the following: drums, bass guitar, or piano/organ * A strong Christian faith and personal commitment to Christ * Understanding of worship ministry and church environment * Reliability and punctuality * Ability to work well within a team **Schedule:** * **Saturday:** Rehearsal (2 hours) * **Sunday:** 9:45 AM – 12:30 PM (Service) **Compensation:** * €20 per hour * Approx. €4,200 per year **Selection Process:** * Submission of application with a brief introduction * A 1\-minute video introducing yourself and demonstrating your musical skill * Audition and interview for shortlisted candidates **How to Apply:** Please apply with a short introduction about yourself, your musical experience, and your faith journey, along with your video submission. Faith City Church is committed to building a dedicated and passionate worship team to serve our community. Job Type: Part\-time Pay: €4,000\.00\-€5,000\.00 per year Benefits: * On\-site parking Work Location: In person

title: Cloud Engineer
description: **Cloud Ops Tech Lead** **Job Description:** Our client, a leading Fintech organisation within the financial services sector, is seeking an experienced Cloud Operations Technical Lead to drive cloud strategy, operational excellence, and team leadership across their technology function. The client is one of the best employers to work for in Ireland. **Job Responsibilities** * Lead and mentor a team of cloud engineers delivering scalable infrastructure solutions * Define and execute cloud strategies aligned with wider business and technology objectives * Ensure high availability, performance, and security across cloud environments * Collaborate with internal teams to integrate cloud services with existing systems and platforms * Act as a key point of contact between technical teams and business stakeholders, ensuring clear communication of priorities and delivery timelines * Support incident management processes, including participation in on\-call activities * Drive optimisation initiatives to improve system performance and cost efficiency * Oversee build, release, and configuration management of production environments * Manage third\-party vendors and cloud service providers * Promote continuous improvement across cloud operations, tooling, and delivery practices **Experience Required** * Minimum of 7\+ years’ experience in cloud engineering or infrastructure roles * Proven experience in a leadership or technical lead capacity * Strong hands\-on experience with major cloud platforms such as AWS and Azure * Demonstrated experience implementing infrastructure\-as\-code solutions (e.g. Terraform) * Experience designing and maintaining CI/CD pipelines within modern DevOps environments * Strong understanding of cloud architecture, operational practices, and service management * Experience working in environments with defined SLAs/SLOs and service delivery expectations **Desirable Skills** * Exposure to DevOps tooling and automation frameworks * Experience with monitoring, logging, and optimisation tools within cloud environments * Understanding of cloud cost management and optimisation strategies * Familiarity with cloud security principles and governance frameworks * Experience working within complex, enterprise\-scale environments * Knowledge of Azure DevOps or similar platforms **Educational Requirements** * Third\-level degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related discipline **Working Hours \& Benefits** * Competitive salary up to €100,000 * Performance\-related bonus * Pension contribution * Flexible and hybrid working options * Opportunities for career progression and professional development **HOW TO APPLY:** If you are interested in this role, please apply with your updated CV

title: Cloud Security Architect.
description: At AIB, our values guide how we work and how we support each other. We’re looking for someone who puts **Customer First** , takes initiative and **Owns the Outcome** , and is always looking for ways to **Eliminate Complexity** . You’ll treat colleagues and customers with fairness and **Show Respect** , and you’ll thrive in a culture built on collaboration where we **Be One Team** to deliver meaningful impact. **Location/Office Policy:** Central Park, Leopardstown, Dublin 18 – Hybrid (3 days per week in office) * Want to play a key role in shaping the future of cloud security in a dynamic, fast\-paced organisation? * Are you a visionary in secure cloud architectures across public, private and hybrid environments? * Can you design cloud solutions that balance business agility with robust security controls? **What Is The Role** Reporting to the Head of Cloud Security, the job holder will provide support within the Cloud Security team, contributing to the engineering of security technologies to safeguard the Bank’s IT systems, network, and applications from current and emerging threats during development and maintenance cycles, with a primary focus on the organization’s cloud environments. The successful candidate will help implement security strategy and target security architectures, patterns, and roadmaps. The successful candidate will work closely with Security Product owners and project teams, supporting the alignment of our applications, products and services to our architectural and security targets as well. The role holder will be responsible for working across various cloud platforms, including public, private, and hybrid models, to assess risks and ensure that cloud controls safeguard the organization’s critical assets and sensitive data. They will be responsible for ensuring that the Security Products are aligned to targets and security architecture will be responsible for the roadmap of those targets. **Key Accountabilities** * Design cloud security processes, including risk and posture management, incident response, and operational resilience, ensuring successful and timely delivery of results * Design and implement robust security processes and frameworks to support cloud adoption, ensure compliance with ISO 27001, NIST, and CIS Benchmarks * Establish a framework for the development, review and implementation of technical security policies, ensuring alignment with regulatory and organisational requirements * Translate AIB’s cloud security strategy and business objectives into secure by design solutions. Maintain secure architectures for public, private, and hybrid cloud environments * Partner with cloud vendors, partner, architects, DevOps teams, and cybersecurity specialists to ensure secure solution delivery * Identify and solve complex security problems, recommending innovative solutions and applying in\-depth technical expertise to protect critical assets and sensitive data * Act as a highly specialised individual contributor providing expertise to stakeholders on cloud security related topics, mentor and guide junior engineers, fostering a culture of technical excellence and continuous learning * Provide technical expertise within the Cloud Working Group and Architecture Review Board, developing and presenting strategic technology solutions to safeguard the Bank’s IT systems, network, and applications * Champion cloud security, compliance, and resilience, embedding controls and monitoring in all solution * Drive cost optimisation (FinOps) and resource efficiency across cloud environments **What You Will Bring** * Minimum 8\+ years in IT security, with 3\+ years in cloud security architecture * Strong experience with cloud platforms (Azure, AWS) and their security services * Strong experience with cloud native technologies (AKS, EKS, OpenShift. Istio, Cilium) and their security services * Proficiency in DevSecOps practices and CI/CD security integration * Deep understanding of encryption standards, key management, and IAM * Knowledge of compliance frameworks (CIS, NIST, ISO27001\) **Preferred Certifications** * CISSP (Certified Information Security Systems Professional) * CCSP (Certified Cloud Security Professional) * CCSK (Certificate of Cloud Security Knowledge) * Azure Security Engineer Associate or AWS Security Specialty * Azure Solution Architecture or AWS Solution Architecture **Why Work For AIB** We are committed to offering our colleagues choice and flexibility in how we work and live and our hybrid working model enables our people to balance their time between working from home and their designated office, subject to their role, the needs of our customers and business requirements. *Some of our benefits include;* * Market leading Pension Scheme * Healthcare Scheme * Variable Pay * Employee Assistance Programme * Family leave options * Two volunteer days per year Please click **here** for further information about AIB’s PACT – Our Commitment to You. **Key Capabilities** * Leads Others * Instils Trust * Customer Focus * Information System Security * Technical Leadership If you are not sure about your suitability based on any aspects of the role advertised, we encourage you to please contact the recruiter Sarah Lyons at *careers@aib.ie* for a conversation. AIB is an equal opportunities employer, and we pride ourselves on being the first bank in Ireland to receive the Investors in Diversity Gold Standard accreditation from the Irish Centre for Diversity. We are committed to providing reasonable accommodations for applicants and employees. Should you have a reasonable accommodation request please email the Talent Acquisition team at careers@aib.ie **Application Deadline: Friday 1st May 2026**

title: Cloud Software Engineer
description: Our Dublin based client are looking for a **Senior Cloud Software Engineer** to join the team on a permanent basis. Excellent career opportunity with an established company that operate on a global scale Hybrid working model with 2 days per week on site **Job Responsibilities** * The implementation of microservices and serverless solutions. * Lead the backend development across various services, ensuring systems are high\-performing, and scalable. * Implement security best practices, including encryption and secure API development. * Work closely with frontend developers, engineers, and business stakeholders * Ensure the backend solutions align with user needs and business goals * Define and uphold coding standards by introducing best practices, building a unified codebase, and implementing comprehensive testing. * Monitor and optimize all aspects of product performance. * Troubleshoot issues across a wide array of services and functional areas. * Contribute to the DevOps culture and practices. * Provide technical leadership and mentorship to junior team members and engineers. **Experience Required** * 5\+ years of experience in backend development and 2 years in a cloud environment. * Highly capable in cloud platforms like AWS, Azure, or GCP * Experience with microservices architecture, RESTful APIs, and containerization technologies. * Track\-record for building robust and scalable applications. * Ability to collaborate effectively, give and receive feedback. Ability to articulate complex technical ideas in an understandable manner **Essential and Desirable Skills** * Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related field. * Experience with architecture, design, and development – experience on the whole life cycle of the product. * Extensive experience with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, Google Cloud). * Proficiency in at least one backend programming language. * Strong understanding of cloud infrastructure, containerization (Docker), and orchestration (Kubernetes). * Strong fundamentals in database systems, SQL DB or No SQL DB – experience dealing with large volumes of data. * Knowledge of high\-scale stream/batch processing systems * Experience managing and maintaining production systems * Expertise in RESTful and GraphQL APIs. **HOW TO APPLY:** To apply for this role, please submit your updated CV. If your skills and experience match job requirements, you will be contacted to discuss your application in detail. **SIMILAR ROLES OPEN:** As a specialized recruitment agency, we also have several other permanent and contract IT roles available. Visit our website for more information or explore additional opportunities.

title: Cloud Solutions Architect
description: **ROLE DESCRIPTION: Job Overview:** We are actively searching for a highly skilled and innovative Cloud Solutions Architect to enhance our esteemed IT consulting services company. The successful candidate will be a seasoned professional with extensive experience in cloud technologies and DevOps practices. In this pivotal role, you will be at the forefront of designing and implementing state\-of\-the\-art solutions that cater to the evolving needs of our diverse clientele. **Key Responsibilities:** * Take a lead role in defining and driving the overall cloud architecture strategy for our clients. * Provide visionary and expert guidance to cross\-functional teams on best practices in cloud and DevOps. * Collaborate closely with clients to understand their unique business challenges and develop tailored cloud solutions. * Design, develop, and oversee the implementation of scalable and secure cloud architectures that align with client goals. * Bring expertise in integrating DevOps methodologies seamlessly into cloud solutions, fostering efficient development pipelines. * Advocate for and implement automation processes to streamline deployment, monitoring, and infrastructure management. Lead technical discussions and present recommendations to clients and internal teams. * Act as a trusted advisor to clients, offering insights into the latest advancements in cloud technologies and recommending strategies to optimize their IT infrastructure. * Stay at the forefront of industry trends, regularly updating your knowledge on emerging technologies and practices in cloud computing. **Minimum Qualifications:** * Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Information Technology, or a related field. * **15\-20 years of overall IT experience.** * **Minimum of 8\-10 years of relevant experience in cloud architecture and DevOps** . * Proven expertise in designing and implementing cloud solutions in AWS and Azure. * Strong understanding of DevOps principles and practices. * Experience with containerization and orchestration tools (e.g., Docker, Kubernetes). * Designing and deploying AI solutions on AWS using AWS Bedrock, Sagemaker etc. **Preferred Qualifications:** * Master’s degree in computer science, Information Technology, or a related field. * Relevant certifications in cloud platforms and DevOps tools. * Proven experience in an IT consulting services environment. * Excellent analytical and problem\-solving skills. * Strong communication and presentation abilities. * Demonstrated leadership in guiding cross\-functional teams. * Familiarity with automation tools for infrastructure provisioning and configuration management.

title: Cloud Solutions Architect (Daily Rate)
description: **Cloud Solutions Architect** **Daily Rate Contract (1 day onsite, Dublin City Centre) Ireland or UK** My Dublin based client is seeking an experienced Cloud Solutions Architect for a daily rate contract. The Cloud Architect will support a large\-scale digital and cloud transformation programme. This is a daily rate contract role focused on designing, delivering, and optimising secure, scalable cloud infrastructure solutions across AWS and/or Azure environments. You will operate within a Cloud Centre of Excellence style function, contributing hands\-on architecture expertise, establishing best practice patterns, and accelerating cloud adoption across enterprise platforms. **Key Responsibilities** * Design and deliver secure, scalable, and cost\-efficient cloud architecture solutions aligned to enterprise and regulatory requirements * Refine and translate business requirements into robust technical designs * Provide strategic guidance on cloud adoption, platform optimisation, and architectural best practice * Drive innovation and cost optimisation across public cloud environments * Present architectural solutions to both technical and non\-technical stakeholders * Ensure security\-by\-design principles are embedded across all solutions * Develop reusable, repeatable cloud patterns to accelerate delivery and reduce platform overhead * Collaborate with engineering teams to implement, deploy, and enhance cloud capabilities * Ensure solutions meet performance, reliability, and governance standards **Required Experience** * 5\+ years’ experience designing and implementing scalable cloud solutions * Strong background in modern cloud\-native architecture (microservices, serverless, containerisation) * Hands\-on Infrastructure as Code expertise (Terraform, CloudFormation) * Deep experience with at least one major cloud platform (AWS or Azure) * Experience designing or implementing Kubernetes solutions (preferably EKS or equivalent) * Proven ability to manage and optimise public cloud infrastructure environments * Strong stakeholder engagement and communication skills * Experience producing clear, structured architecture and design documentation **Desirable Experience** * Exposure to regulated or complex enterprise environments * Experience across both AWS and Azure * CI/CD and DevOps implementation using IaC tooling * Experience supporting cloud migration or transformation programmes **Certifications (Advantageous)** * AWS Certified Solutions Architect (or equivalent cloud certification)

title: Cloud Support Engineer
description: **Job Description:** Our client, a leading financial services technology organisation, is seeking a Senior Cloud Support Engineer to join their service delivery function, supporting and maintaining a global cloud\-based platform. **Job Responsibilities** * Provide high\-quality technical support to end users, ensuring service levels and customer satisfaction targets are consistently achieved * Deploy, manage and optimise cloud\-based infrastructure to ensure performance, scalability and security across production environments * Build and maintain CI/CD pipelines using modern tooling to support efficient software delivery * Monitor system performance, availability and capacity, proactively addressing issues and improving resilience * Manage system upgrades, patching activities and configuration changes across environments * Act as an escalation point for complex technical issues and drive timely resolution * Collaborate with cross\-functional teams to deliver reliable and scalable technical solutions * Develop and maintain infrastructure as code using tools such as Terraform * Implement monitoring, logging and observability practices across platforms * Support incident, problem and change management processes aligned to ITIL frameworks * Drive continuous improvement through automation, optimisation and operational best practices **Experience Required** * Minimum 5 years’ experience in cloud infrastructure, technical operations or a similar role * Proven experience supporting highly available, 24/7 cloud\-based environments * Strong hands\-on experience with cloud platforms, particularly Microsoft Azure, alongside exposure to AWS * Experience with cloud\-native architectures and distributed systems * Demonstrated experience in customer\-facing support roles within technical environments * Strong troubleshooting and problem\-solving skills across infrastructure and application layers * Experience with monitoring and alerting tools within cloud environments * Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines and deployment tooling such as Azure DevOps * Experience with identity and access management principles, including role\-based access controls * Proven ability to manage incidents effectively and maintain composure in high\-pressure situations **Desirable Skills** * Experience with platform security best practices and secure\-by\-design principles * Knowledge of vulnerability management and remediation approaches * Exposure to infrastructure automation and configuration management tools * Experience implementing observability frameworks including logging and metrics collection * Understanding of modern DevOps practices and continuous delivery models * Strong communication skills with the ability to work across global teams **Educational Requirements** * Bachelor’s degree or equivalent in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related discipline **Working Hours \& Benefits** * Competitive salary and benefits package * Opportunity to work within a global, technology\-driven environment * Exposure to modern cloud\-native technologies and large\-scale platforms * Collaborative and supportive team culture with opportunities for professional growth **How to Apply** To apply for this role, please submit your updated CV. If your skills and experience match job requirements, you will be contacted to discuss your application in detail. **Similar Roles Open** As a specialised recruitment agency, we also have several other permanent and contract IT roles available. Visit our website for more information or explore additional opportunities.

title: Commercial & Finance Coordinator
description: **Job Description:** **Commercial \& Finance Coordinator (Hybrid)** **Airbus International Services, Dublin, Ireland** Airbus is a global leader in aeronautics, space and related services. Airbus offers the most comprehensive range of passenger airliners from 100 to more than 600 seats and business aviation products. Airbus is also a European leader providing tanker, combat, transport and mission aircraft, as well as one of the world’s leading space companies. In helicopters, Airbus provides the most efficient civil and military rotor\-craft solutions worldwide. Airbus International Services Airbus International Services DAC (AIS), is a wholly owned subsidiary of Airbus Helicopters, dedicated to the management of external technical assistance for Airbus clients. With around 100 headcount, our employees are based in jurisdictions across the world, with the management team located in Dublin, Ireland. Join the Commercial team Airbus International Services is a wholly owned subsidiary of Airbus Helicopters, which provides resource based solutions to Airbus customers across the world. Based in Dublin, the Commercial team supports just under 100 globally mobile employees. Roles and Responsibilities Commercial Support: Assist in the preparation, issuance, and tracking of commercial proposals and amendments in line with company pricing policies. Procurement Support: Assist in the posting of GR’s, creation of PO’s and ad hoc tasks . Mission Coordination: Act as the point of contact for mission preparation, including issuing mission numbers, managing travel bookings, and handling expense platforms. Financial Administration: Support the Commercial and Finance Teams with billing, invoice queries, reconciliations, and the management of customer Purchase Orders (POs) . Data Management: Maintain absolute accuracy of commercial and financial data within internal information systems and CRM tools. Operational Liaison: Provide support and assistance to the Commercial Project Managers \& Finance Team on their daily activities. Reporting: Assist in the delivery of monthly pipeline forecasts and cost target monitoring. Experience * A third\-level business degree is required * 2\+ years in an office administration role Skills * Strong proficiency in Excel and CRM systems; high attention to detail and numerical accuracy * Strong coordination of multiple internal departments and third\-party providers * High level of confidentiality and discretion required * Excellent attention to detail and the ability to spot anomalies * Ability to multitask and prioritise their workload * Excellent communication skills \& ability to work on their own initiative * Problem solving skills * Ability to meet deadlines and take ownership of the role * Team spirit and positive attitude to work are key: proactive attitude * Proficient level in Microsoft Office Suite, in particular Microsoft Excel and Google Suite * SAP experience desirable but not essential Communication: Ability to liaise effectively across multiple internal departments. Organisation: Proven ability to manage multiple deadlines and prioritise daily deliverables in a fast\-paced environment. This job requires an awareness of any potential compliance risks and a commitment to act with integrity, as the foundation for the Company’s success, reputation and sustainable growth. **Company:** Airbus International Services Designated Activity Company **Employment Type:** Permanent \- **Experience Level:** Entry Level **Job Family:** Sales, Marketing \& Commercial Contracts \<JF\-MC\-VE\> By submitting your CV or application you are consenting to Airbus using and storing information about you for monitoring purposes relating to your application or future employment. This information will only be used by Airbus. Airbus is committed to achieving workforce diversity and creating an inclusive working environment. We welcome all applications irrespective of social and cultural background, age, gender, disability, sexual orientation or religious belief. Airbus is, and always has been, committed to equal opportunities for all. As such, we will never ask for any type of monetary exchange in the frame of a recruitment process. Any impersonation of Airbus to do so should be reported to emsom@airbus.com . At Airbus, we support you to work, connect and collaborate more easily and flexibly. Wherever possible, we foster flexible working arrangements to stimulate innovative thinking.

title: Commercial Graduate Rotation Program - UK & Ireland Markets
description: *To get the best candidate experience, please consider applying for a maximum of 3 roles within 12 months to ensure you are not duplicating efforts.* **About Futureforce University Recruiting** Our Futureforce University Recruiting program is dedicated to attracting, retaining and cultivating talent. Our interns and new graduates work on real projects that affect how our business runs, giving them the opportunity to make a tangible impact on the future of our company. With offices all over the world, our recruits have the chance to collaborate and connect with fellow employees on a global scale. We offer job shadowing, mentorship programs, talent development courses, and much more. **Job Category** Sales **Job Details** **About Salesforce** Salesforce is the \#1 AI CRM, where humans with agents drive customer success together. Here, ambition meets action. Tech meets trust. And innovation isn’t a buzzword — it’s a way of life. The world of work as we know it is changing and we're looking for Trailblazers who are passionate about bettering business and the world through AI, driving innovation, and keeping Salesforce's core values at the heart of it all. Ready to level\-up your career at the company leading workforce transformation in the agentic era? You’re in the right place! Agentforce is the future of AI, and you are the future of Salesforce. **Start Date: August 2026** The EMEA Graduate Rotation Program is a new and exciting 12 month program which welcomes ambitious graduates who would like to begin their career journey within sales and solution engineering. It is a rotational program designed to give graduates exposure to both sales development and solution engineering. Having built the foundational skills and passed all required eligibility and readiness criteria, our graduates can then go on to pursue one of these two clear career paths. Our Top Graduates can potentially promote to the career path of their choice, (depending on business demand) by month 7 of the program. Our graduate program is a fully immersive program combining comprehensive learning paths, in\-role experience, customer interaction and mentorship from senior sales and solution engineering professionals. At Salesforce, you will have the opportunity to lay the foundations for a successful career in sales \& solution engineering and accelerate your career at a fast pace. We are a proud member of *Fortune 100* *Best Places to Work* list for the past 12 years and a consistent winner of *Great Places to Work.* **Who are we looking for?** * You are a 2025 or 2026 graduate (minimum bachelor’s degree). We consider applicants from All Academic Fields * You are interested in the impact technology can have on the world and business * You are a creative problem\-solver * You are a relationship builder and possess a growth mindset * Are curious, self\-motivated and focused on continuous learning * You thrive working in a team environment * You have excellent interpersonal skills * You are goal\-orientated and comfortable with a fast\-paced environment * You are fluent in English **What Does Our New EMEA Graduate Program Look Like** The Program is structured as follows: **Introduction Month** During your first month you will have extensive onboarding enablement in a Salesforce\-like academy, where you will: * Get immersed in the culture of salesforce and truly understand our values * Develop an understanding of our solutions, technologies and tools. * Learn critical professional skills for your career in salesforce * Structured learning calendar with sessions \& hands\-on workshops led by experts and program alumni **Rotations** During this Graduate Program you will have the opportunity to experience **the Sales Graduate Path** and **the Solution Engineering Graduate Path.** The structure for both are similar, for both you will spend 60% of your time immersed in the role, you will have clear tasks, clear targets and you will align with key stakeholders and the other 40% of your time will be spent on role specific learning. You will be paired with mentors/buddies with experience in the path. If during your first rotation (months 2 throughout to month 4\) are on the Solution Engineering Graduate Rotation, then you will join the Sales Graduate Rotation (from month 5 through to 7\) and vice versa. So in the these 6 months you will have exposure to both roles. Your time spent mastering the fundamentals of both these roles during these rotations will empower you to be even more productive and impactful when you eventually promote. Throughout the rotations there will be self paced certification and skills assessment. You will be required to meet all readiness eligibility criteria before you can Panel for Promotion. **An Example of what you can expect during the specific rotations** **The Sales Graduate Rotation** * You will be taught salesforce’s best in class prospecting and qualification methodologies * You will learn how to use social, digital, video, chat and phone platforms to effectively engage with Salesforce prospects * You will collaborative with Sales Development Specialists to run campaigns and help build sales pipeline for the EMEA Sales organisation **The Solution Engineer Graduate Rotation** * You will have the opportunity to learn our solutions at a deeper more technical level * You work closely with the solution engineering organisation supporting them in building cutting edge cutomer demonstrations * You will learn the art of customer discovery \& how to create a customer point of view **What’s in it for you?** * A clear and structured career path within our Sales and Solution Engineering organisation * Opportunity to work in an international team, within a nurturing environment * Opportunity to join our diverse internal employee groups * Comprehensive benefits package * Relocation budget and guidance in your relocation\-process * Additional seven days paid time off for charitable causes \- check out our 1\-1\-1 model **Map Your Future** With our Graduate Program you will have the choice of career paths * You can choose to take a Sales Path where after your Grad Program Rotations you can progress to Sales Development Representative, Business Development Representative and then into our Sales organisation as Account Executive, where you will position our solutions to potential customers. In some instances, this path may vary or be subject to change. * You can choose to take a Solution Engineering path where after your Grad Program Rotations you can progress to Associate Solution Engineer and rise to work at every customer level ESB, SMB, GRB. In some instances, this path may vary or be subject to change. * With our strong culture of mentoring we strive to develop industry\-leading sales and solution engineering leaders. This is a phenomenal opportunity to develop your international experience in an organisation that has transformed the Enterprise Software Market with Cloud Computing **Salesforce in Dublin** When we opened our first office in Ireland in 2000 we were one of the first digital companies to locate here. Today, our Dublin office has grown into a leading technology hub, and Salesforce is the global leader in customer relationship management (CRM), bringing companies of every size and industry together with their customers. Our new Salesforce Tower Dublin will bring our dynamic, multinational team together in the vibrant Silicon Docks area. **Accommodations** If you require assistance due to a disability applying for open positions please submit a request via this Accommodations Request Form. **Posting Statement** At Salesforce we believe that the business of business is to improve the state of our world. Each of us has a responsibility to drive Equality in our communities and workplaces. We are committed to creating a workforce that reflects society through inclusive programs and initiatives such as equal pay, employee resource groups, inclusive benefits, and more. Learn more about Equality at Salesforce and explore our benefits. Salesforce.com and Salesforce.org are Equal Employment Opportunity and Affirmative Action Employers. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender perception or identity, national origin, age, marital status, protected veteran status, or disability status. Salesforce.com and Salesforce.org do not accept unsolicited headhunter and agency resumes. Salesforce.com and Salesforce.org will not pay any third\-party agency or company that does not have a signed agreement with Salesforce.com or Salesforce.org. Salesforce welcomes all. Unleash Your Potential When you join Salesforce, you’ll be limitless in all areas of your life. Our benefits and resources support you to find balance and *be your best* , and our AI agents accelerate your impact so you can *do your best* . Together, we’ll bring the power of Agentforce to organizations of all sizes and deliver amazing experiences that customers love. Apply today to not only shape the future — but to redefine what’s possible — for yourself, for AI, and the world. Accommodations If you need a reasonable accommodation during the application or the recruiting process, please submit a request via this Accommodations Request Form. Please note that Salesforce uses artificial intelligence (AI) tools to help our recruiters assess and evaluate candidates’ resumes and qualifications throughout the recruiting process. Humans will always make any candidate selection and hiring decisions. Please see our Candidate Privacy Statement for more information about how we use your personal data and your rights, including with regard to use of AI tools and opt out options. ********Posting Statement******** Salesforce is an equal opportunity employer and maintains a policy of non\-discrimination with all employees and applicants for employment. What does that mean exactly? It means that at Salesforce, we believe in equality for all. And we believe we can lead the path to equality in part by creating a workplace that’s inclusive, and free from discrimination. Know your rights: workplace discrimination is illegal. Any employee or potential employee will be assessed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications – without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender expression or identity, transgender status, age, disability, veteran or marital status, political viewpoint, or other classifications protected by law. This policy applies to current and prospective employees, no matter where they are in their Salesforce employment journey. It also applies to recruiting, hiring, job assignment, compensation, promotion, benefits, training, assessment of job performance, discipline, termination, and everything in between. Recruiting, hiring, and promotion decisions at Salesforce are fair and based on merit. The same goes for compensation, benefits, promotions, transfers, reduction in workforce, recall, training, and education.
