title: Tax Analyst (Big 4 Recently Qualified)
description: **Tax Analyst (Big 4 Recently Qualified) \- Global Manufacturing Group \- Dublin** A global manufacturing group is seeking to appoint a Tax Analyst to join its finance team in Dublin. Operating across multiple international locations, the business is a leader in its industry. This role offers exposure to a broad range of tax and finance activities in a dynamic, cross\-border environment. **The Role** Working as part of a collaborative international finance team, you will support tax compliance, reporting, and planning activities across multiple jurisdictions, while partnering with stakeholders globally. **Key Responsibilities** * Assist in the preparation and review of corporate tax filings and submissions * Contribute to transfer pricing analysis and supporting documentation * Track regulatory changes and evaluate their potential impact on the business * Support tax audits and act as a point of contact for external advisors and revenue authorities * Assist in implementing tax planning strategies and efficiency initiatives * Prepare and review tax provisions in accordance with relevant accounting standards * Undertake research on tax issues and produce clear, concise reports * Work closely with internal teams to ensure compliance and drive process improvements **About You** * Big 4 Recently Qualified Tax professional * Strong understanding of domestic and international tax regulations * Strong communication skills and ability to work across teams **Why Apply?** * Exposure to a global, multi\-entity environment * Strong learning and development opportunities * Collaborative, international team culture * Opportunity to work in a sustainability\-focused organisation **What’s on Offer:** * Competitive salary up to €70,000 DOE, Bonus, Pension, and a hybrid working model (2 days WFH). **If this role is of interest to you, please feel free to reach out to me at Dean.Kennedy@hireforce.ie. If you know of any friends or colleagues who might suit this role and would like to refer them, you will receive a €250 voucher should we successfully place them.**

title: Technical Business Analyst
description: **Technical Business Analyst – Greenfield Opportunity** ● Own delivery from requirements through to implementation ● Work closely with customers and engineering teams ● Shape data structure, reporting, and visualisation Join a high\-growth SaaS company transforming how organisations use data to make decisions. title: Technical Business Analyst – 6- Month Contract
description: **Technical Business Analyst – 6\- Month Contract, South Dublin** Don’t miss out on this great opportunity. Apply today for an immediate start!!! **Technical Business Analyst Role and Responsibilities** : Our client is seeking a Technical Business Analyst to support the enhancement of Finance and IT control environments. This role is key to strengthening governance, improving risk visibility, and supporting compliance. You will work across critical initiatives including Privileged Access Monitoring (PAM), Segregation of Duties (SoD), and Log Gap Analysis—partnering with technology, risk, and business teams to deliver control improvements. **What you’ll do:** * Gather and document business and technical requirements across IT control initiatives * Analyse privileged access, user roles, and system logs to identify control gaps and risk exposure * Conduct Segregation of Duties (SoD) assessments and support remediation planning * Perform log gap analysis across systems to ensure adequate monitoring and compliance * Translate complex technical findings into clear, actionable insights and documentation * Facilitate workshops with stakeholders across Technology, Risk, and Business teams * Support audit and regulatory requirements through high\-quality reporting and evidence **What you need:** * Experience in technology risk, IT controls, security, or governance programmes * Strong analytical skills with the ability to interpret access models, logs, and technical data * Experience working with datasets (Excel, SQL, or similar tools) * Understanding of privileged access, IAM, SIEM, or logging frameworks * Strong stakeholder engagement and communication skills Desirable skills * Experience with IAM/PAM tools (e.g., CyberArk) * Knowledge of SoD frameworks or SOX controls * Exposure to cloud platforms and logging/monitoring tools **What’s on offer:** * Competitive daily rate depending on experience * Hybrid working model\- 2 days on site * High chance of contract renewal **Apply Now** If you would like to discuss this opportunity in person or with one of our IT Resourcers please forward your CV to Vantage Resources or contact Eric Seery on eric.seery@vantage.ie for a confidential discussion. Vantage Resources will not forward your details without prior discussion and approval. Vantage Resources is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive equal consideration for engagement and/or employment. An inclusive and diverse workforce is an essential part of the development of our organisation’s culture which we believe enhances both our working environment and the service we provide to our customers.

title: Test Analyst
description: #### **Company Description** The Royal College of Physicians of Ireland (RCPI), with its Faculties and Institutes, provides postgraduate medical training and professional development opportunities to doctors working in 25 specialties. Collectively, we are the largest provider of postgraduate medical training in Ireland. Our mission is to ensure that patients receive the best possible care. We do this by training and assessing doctors and providing accredited education and professional development programmes. We also quality‑assure standards in specialist medical practice and promote good health through our policy and public outreach initiatives. Our members and senior management team are heavily involved in the current transformation of the Irish health service. We work closely with the Medical Council of Ireland, the Health Service Executive, the Department of Health, and other postgraduate medical training bodies. We are also undergoing significant internal changes. We are implementing a major programme of transformation that has seen the creation of new business initiatives, the internationalisation of the organisation and closer collaboration with key policy leaders in healthcare. We are continuing to adopt a more proactive and progressive role in the Irish and international health sector and have become a more outward‑looking organisation in recent years as a result. As new initiatives have been developed, our workforce has grown, and we now employ approximately 180 staff. We value creativity and innovation and encourage our staff to ‘think outside the box’. Our staff surveys consistently show that the Royal College of Physicians of Ireland is a great place to work, with a friendly, supportive atmosphere and a sense that everyone is playing their part in improving Irish healthcare. RCPI’s vision is “A globally connected community of doctors improving people’s health”, and RCPI’s mission is to develop and support our learners, trainees, members and fellows to be leaders in healthcare delivery and transformation through training, education and advocacy. **The Connect Programme** The “Connect Programme” is a large transformational initiative that will enable people, processes, technology and organisational structures to develop capabilities to meet our mission. The programme is driven by a sense of urgency, is broad in scope and impact, and will be delivered in parallel with ongoing operations. The proposed change initiative and software solution upgrade aim to enable the transformation of the College. This transformation will be enabled by changes in process and structure to critical College capabilities, including business development and recruitment, admissions/applications, registry and record management, training/education delivery and services delivery. These transformation changes will allow the College to enhance communication with prospective trainees, learners, members and fellows (TLMF) and applicants, and improve overall administrative efficiency. This strategic initiative seeks to establish RCPI as a leading institution in healthcare education and leadership by providing a seamless and personalised experience for all stakeholders. **Progress to date*** Programme governance structure in place * Programme and project teams in place * Organisation design structure updated * As‑Is and To‑Be mapping complete * Business requirements defined and documented * Some readiness projects complete * Procurement partner selection complete **Current Status*** Procurement activities commencing * Discovery and validation phase commencing * Implementation readiness projects underway #### **Job Description** **Role:** Test Analyst **Duration:** 2\-year fixed\-term contract **Salary:** 47,000 \+ Benefits **Closing Date:** 1 May 2026 at 16:00 The Test Analyst will support the successful delivery of the RCPI Connect Programme by designing, executing, and reporting on testing activities to ensure that new and enhanced digital services meet business requirements, quality standards, and regulatory obligations. Working as part of the Connect testing team, the Test Analyst will collaborate closely with programme workstreams, business stakeholders, IT teams, and external vendors to verify that solutions are fit for purpose, reliable, and ready for operational use. The role reports to the Test Manager and works alongside Test Leads and other Test Analysts across the Connect Programme, partnering with Business Analysts, developers, DevOps, and external vendors. **Key Deliverables (first 3–6 months):** * Traceability in place between requirements/acceptance criteria and test cases. * Test cases and regression pack drafted/updated for allocated workstreams. * Functional and integration test execution evidence captured and available for audit. * Defects logged with clear reproduction steps, prioritised via triage, and re\-tested to closure. * UAT support pack prepared (guidance, scripts, and defect support) and UAT outcomes captured. * Regular status updates provided to the Test Manager/Test Lead, including risks and blockers. **Key Responsibilities:** **Test Design \& Preparation*** Analyse business and functional requirements to ensure they are clear, testable, and aligned to intended outcomes. * Design and maintain test cases and test scripts that are traceable back to requirements and acceptance criteria. * Work with the Test Manager and Test Leads to identify testing risks, issues, and dependencies. * Contribute to test approach discussions (e.g., agile/hybrid delivery, shift\-left testing) and ensure test coverage aligns to user stories, acceptance criteria, and planned releases. * Coordinate test data and environment needs with IT/DevOps and vendors (e.g., access, refreshes, data masking), escalating constraints and risks as needed. **Test Execution*** Execute functional (including regression, integration, and system tests) as well as non\-functional where required in line with agreed test plans. * Ensure regression testing is carried out in a risk\-based manner to minimise the introduction of defects. * Support User Acceptance Testing (UAT) activities, working with business users to validate outcomes and resolve issues. * Execute agreed non\-functional checks where required (e.g., accessibility, performance, security, auditability, and data integrity) and support remediation validation. **Defect Management \& Reporting*** Log, track, and manage defects through to resolution using agreed tooling (e.g. Jira or equivalent). * Participate in defect triage discussions with internal teams and external vendors. * Contribute to test progress reporting and test metrics, including execution status and defect trends. **Collaboration \& Continuous Improvement*** Work closely with Connect workstreams, business units, and delivery partners to understand service impacts and delivery priorities. * Share knowledge, templates, and good practices with peers, and support onboarding of new test resources where applicable. * Contribute to the continuous improvement of testing practices, standards, and documentation across the Connect Programme. #### **Requirements** **Essential Experience \& Skills** * 2–3 years’ hands\-on software testing experience, ideally within multi\-phase or complex delivery programmes. * Experience reviewing requirements, specifications, and design documentation to provide meaningful testing input. * Strong test design skills, with experience writing clear, concise, and comprehensive test cases using a test management tool. * Experience executing functional, regression, and integration tests. * Experience managing defects through to resolution using a defect management tool. * Strong attention to detail and a clear commitment to quality. * Ability to plan, prioritise, and report on daily testing activities. * Strong interpersonal skills, with the ability to work collaboratively across multiple teams. **Desirable Experience*** Experience with common QA tooling (e.g., Jira with Xray/Zephyr, Azure DevOps, TestRail, or similar). * Experience in testing data migrations or complex system integrations. * Experience testing enterprise platforms (e.g. CRM, ERP, or integrated digital systems). * Experience working in regulated environments, such as healthcare, education, or professional bodies. * Familiarity with GDPR and data protection considerations in system delivery. **Qualifications*** Degree\-level qualification (NFQ Level 7 or equivalent) in Computer Science, Information Systems, or a related discipline, or equivalent professional experience. * Relevant testing or quality assurance certification desirable but not essential. This is a unique opportunity to contribute to the testing and quality assurance activities of a high\-impact transformation that will shape the future of RCPI. As a Test Analyst on the Connect Programme, you will play an important role in ensuring that new digital services are delivered safely, reliably, and to the highest quality standards. **Competencies:** **1\. Test Analysis \& Design** Translates business and system requirements into clear, structured test scenarios and test cases with appropriate coverage. **2\.** **Test Execution \& Defect Management** Executes tests accurately, records results, and logs, retests, and supports resolution of defects in line with agreed processes. **3\. Quality \& Attention to Detail** Applies a strong quality mindset to identify defects, data issues, and edge cases, ensuring outcomes meet acceptance criteria. 4\. **Collaboration \& Communication** Works effectively with Test Managers, Business Analysts, developers, and business users, clearly communicating progress, risks, and issues. If you are passionate about quality, collaboration, and enabling meaningful outcomes through robust testing and assurance, we would welcome your application. Please submit your CV and a cover letter outlining your software testing experience and your ability to work collaboratively in complex delivery environments.#### **Why us?** **Leave:** We offer 21 days annual leave with an additional 3\-4 college days as well as long service leave to all employees. We also provide support for staff with Maternity, Sick, Parents, and Parental leave. **Wellbeing:** An Employee Assistance Programme with Spectrum Life is available to all staff members as well as annual flu vaccine. **Flexible Hybrid working model:** The RCPI offer a flexible 35\-hour\-a\-week hybrid working model for all employees from their start date, giving staff the autonomy to work from home combined with time spent onsite. **Pension:** A defined contribution pension scheme of 1\.5% Employee contribution and RCPI contribution from commencement date with an increase after 18 months **Flexible Benefit:** Pro\-rata €1,500 annual flexible benefit upon completion of a successful probationary period. **Life Assurance:** 4 times your annual salary. **Learning \& Development:** We offer CPD options including study leave and unlimited access to LinkedIn Learning. **Diversity and Inclusion**: RCPI recognises the importance of equality, diversity, and inclusion. We were recently awarded a Silver accreditation from Investors in Diversity. We are an open and inclusive organisation that celebrates and welcomes diversity. We lead initiatives that support, promote and demonstrate a culture of mutual respect, inclusivity, and diversity, as outlined in our Diversity and Inclusion policy. #### **Accommodations** The Royal College of Physicians of Ireland is an inclusive employer and in line with our policies you are invited to disclose any reasonable accommodations you require for applying, interviewing or working with RCPI. Please be assured this information will be treated as confidential and not used for the purpose of selection. If you have any queries, please contact Suzann Donnelly at suzanndonnelly@rcpi.ie. #### **About us** The Royal College of Physicians of Ireland is a postgraduate medical training college dedicated to ensuring that doctors have the skills to provide patients with the best possible care. Founded in 1654, the college supports doctors to enhance their skills, competencies, and professionalism throughout their working lives. Through its Faculties and Institutes, The Royal College of Physicians of Ireland, offers medical training programmes, examinations, diplomas, and educational courses to equip doctors with the skills and knowledge they need to care for patients and run safe and efficient healthcare systems. It also offers a range of educational diplomas and courses for learners in the wider healthcare sector.

title: Trust & Safety New Associate - Spanish
description: **Position:** Trust and Safety New Associate \- Spanish \- CL13 **Location:** Dublin, Ireland \- Eastpoint D3 **Contract** : Full\-time role 40 hours per week, 12 month contract (with view to extension) **Work Hours** : Must be flexible to work in a 24/7 environment. Accenture is a trusted, innovative, comprehensive, and experienced partner to leading platform companies. The Trust and Safety offering within Accenture Operations helps keep the internet safe and helps platform companies accelerate, scale, and improve their businesses.. Content moderators serve as an important line of defense by helping improve the quality and safety of online content while gaining hands\-on job experience. You will deliver value to our clients, while making a larger impact on society. You will be responsible for analyzing and reviewing user profiles, audio, videos, and text\-based content and/or investigating, escalating and/or resolving issues that are reported by users or flagged by the system. Due to the nature of the role, the individual may be exposed to flashing lights or contrasting light and dark patterns. Content moderation is meaningful work that helps keep the internet safe. It may also be challenging, at times. In the context of this role, individuals may be directly or inadvertently exposed to potentially objectionable and sensitive content (e.g., graphic, violent, sexual, or egregious). Therefore, content moderators need strong resilience and coping skills. We care for the health and well\-being of our people and provide the support and resources needed to perform their role responsibilities. Active participation in Accenture’s well\-being support program, designed specifically for the Trust \& Safety community, provides valuable skills to promote individual and collective well\-being. **Role Details** - Review, classify and/or remove content according to client guidelines, using specific tools and channels - Understand and remain updated on changing client policies and guidelines - Investigate, resolve, and relay complex content issues to the broader Trust and Safety team - Serve as an advocate for the user community - Participate in process improvement initiatives that improve quality and efficiency of work - Participate in continuous training programs and workgroup discussions for optimal development in the role - Engage in conversation around socially sensitive topics with the purpose of keeping our communities safe **Basic Qualifications/Skills Needed** - Strong coping, emotional resilience, and stress\-management skills - Excellent comprehension, communication, and English language skills - Ability to perform duties objectively, devoid of inherent biases or personal beliefs, in the best interest of client policies - Strong attention to detail - Comfort synthesizing and analyzing information from multiple streams - Strong critical thinking and decision\-making skills - Deep familiarity and passion for the internet, internet platforms, and internet culture - High affinity and cultural awareness of political and social situations regarding the relevant market and region that will be supported - Ability to work well individually and as part of a team - Ability to work differing rotations/shifts and non\-standard work hours - Flexibility in meeting changing business needs in a fast\-paced environment - Commitment to quality, efficiency, and effectiveness - Previous business process outsourcing, customer service, or content moderation experience is a plus - Over 18 years of age - Shift timings/requirements to work weekends and holidays **Benefits:** - Working for an international enterprise company - Great training - Company social events - Opportunities for carrier progression Accenture is an equal opportunities employer and welcomes applications from all sections of society and does not discriminate on grounds of race, religion or belief, ethnic or national origin, disability, age citizenship, marital, domestic or civil partnership status, sexual orientation or gender identity. If you are required to work a night shift as part of this role, you are obliged to complete a night worker health assessment. Please note that it is a requirement of your continued employment, that you are fit to work night shifts. **About Accenture** Accenture is a global professional services company with leading capabilities in digital, cloud and security. Combining unmatched experience and specialized skills across more than 40 industries, we offer Strategy and Consulting, Interactive, Technology and Operations services — all powered by the world’s largest network of Advanced Technology and Intelligent Operations centers. Our 674,000 people deliver on the promise of technology and human ingenuity every day, serving clients in more than 120 countries. We embrace the power of change to create value and shared success for our clients, people, shareholders, partners and communities. Visit us at accenture.com. **Disclaimer** Content moderation is meaningful work that may be challenging at times. In the context of this role, individuals may directly or inadvertently be exposed to potentially objectionable and sensitive content and will need strong resilience and coping skills. We care for the health and well\-being of our people and provide the support and resources needed to execute the responsibilities of their roles. Active participation in Accenture’s well\-being support program, designed specifically for the Trust \& Safety community, provides valuable skills to promote individual and collective well\-being. Due to the nature of the role, the user may be frequently exposed to flashing lights or contrasting light and dark patterns. **INCLUSION AND DIVERSITY** We are a global collective of diverse talents. Innovators improving the way the world works and lives. We provide an accessible, inclusive, open and welcoming workplace that helps everyone make the most of their professional potential. Discover how we are accelerating equality for all in the workplace. Please advise if you have any particular requirements, so we can look into arranging reasonable adjustments to the recruitment process. You can contact us at accessIErecruitment@accenture.com. **As part of the interview process, you will be required to complete an English language test and a language test in the relevant language for this role. You will be advised of the level you are required to achieve to successfully carry out the role.**

title: Utilities Technician
description: **🚧 Utilities Technician \- (Immediately Available) \| North Dublin \| Manufacturing 🚧** A leading manufacturing operation in the North Dublin area is seeking an experienced **Utilities Technician** to join the team on a **rolling contract basis** . If you enjoy hands‑on technical work, troubleshooting, and keeping critical systems running at their best, this could be a fantastic opportunity. **🔧 The Role** We are currently seeking a Utilities Technician available to start at short notice to support a high‑performing manufacturing operation in the North Dublin area. As a key member of the utilities \& engineering team, you’ll support the safe and reliable operation of essential site systems, including: * HVAC * Steam * Water * Gases \& compressed air * Fire protection * Waste systems You’ll monitor, operate, and perform first‑line maintenance on these utilities to keep the site running efficiently and in line with regulatory standards. **📌 What You’ll Be Doing** * Responding to utilities alarms and troubleshooting faults * Performing routine inspections, rounds, and readings * Carrying out preventative \& corrective maintenance * Supporting planned engineering activities with internal teams \& contractors * Following safety processes (permit\-to\-work, LOTO, etc.) * Helping drive reliability and continuous improvement on site **🎓 What We’re Looking For** * Trade qualification in Mechanical, Electrical, Instrumentation, or Marine Engineering * Solid understanding of utilities systems (HVAC, water, steam, gases, fire systems) * Experience in manufacturing, industrial, regulated, or marine environments * Strong troubleshooting and problem‑solving skills * Ability to work both independently and within a multi\-disciplinary team

title: Van Driver/Warehouse Operative
description: **Location:** Rathcoole **Hours:** Monday to Friday, 09:00 – 18:00 (hours may change to suit business needs) **Salary:** €38,675 per annum **About the Role** Elliott Baxter \& Company Ltd (EBB) is seeking a reliable and proactive **Van Driver / Warehouse Operative** to join our busy Dublin distribution team. This is a dual\-role position involving both multidrop van deliveries to our customers across the region and some warehouse duties . We’re looking for someone with great attention to detail, pride in their work, and a strong commitment to safety and customer service. You’ll play a key part in ensuring that our customers receive their orders accurately, safely, and on time \- every time. **Key Responsibilities** **As a Van Driver:** * Carry out daily multidrop deliveries using a van, ensuring all deliveries are made safely, on time, and in full. * Conduct daily vehicle checks and report any defects in line with company policy. * Handle goods carefully during loading, transport, and delivery. * Provide excellent customer service at every drop, representing EBB in a professional manner. * Ensure the vehicle is kept clean, tidy, and roadworthy. * Follow all driving, road safety, and legal compliance standards. **As a Warehouse Operative:** * Accurately pick, scan, and prepare orders in line with company procedures. * Load and unload vehicles safely and efficiently. * Maintain a clean, organised, and safe warehouse environment. * Follow all training and safe systems of work, including manual handling and Health \& Safety requirements. * Complete relevant paperwork or digital records promptly and accurately. * Report any damages, near misses, or hazards to management immediately. **Essential Skills and Experience** * Previous experience in a multidrop delivery and warehouse environment. * Strong focus on customer service and professionalism. * Good communication and interpersonal skills. * Excellent attention to detail. * Physically fit, reliable, and safety\-conscious. * Flexible and team\-oriented attitude. * Knowledge of warehouse management systems (WMS) is beneficial. **Benefits** * Competitive salary. * Monday–Friday schedule. * Flexible holiday scheme. * Cycle to Work scheme. * Ongoing training and development opportunities. * Supportive, people\-focused culture. **About Elliott Baxter \& Co Ltd** Elliott Baxter \& Co Ltd is the UK and Ireland’s leading independent paper and board supplier. With eleven state\-of\-the\-art distribution depots and a modern fleet of vehicles, we pride ourselves on delivering exceptional service and reliability. Our people are at the heart of our success \- professional, friendly, and committed to doing things the right way. Join us and become part of a forward\-thinking business that values its people as much as its customers. Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: Up to €38,675\.00 per year Benefits: * Company pension * On\-site parking Licence/Certification: * Driver Licence (required) Work Location: In person

title: Veterinary Nurse - Small Animal
description: Veterinary Nurse – Small Animal Rathcoole Veterinary Clinic \| South Dublin Full\-time, Permanent Salary €30,000\-€40,000 DOE A Nursing Role With Space to Learn, Grow and Shape Rathcoole Veterinary Clinic is offering a genuinely exciting opportunity for a Veterinary Nurse who wants to feel valued, supported and involved in the future of a growing practice. This is a chance to be part of a close\-knit, friendly team where your voice matters and your ideas are welcomed. You won’t be one of many here – you’ll be a key part of how the clinic runs and develops. The Role This is a varied, hands\-on nursing position covering all aspects of first\-opinion small animal practice. Your day will include clinical nursing, surgical rotations, inpatient care, nurse clinics and client support, to keep the practice running smoothly as a team. Importantly, there is no out\-of\-hours or on\-call work, allowing you to enjoy a healthy and sustainable work–life balance while still working in a busy, rewarding environment. A Practice Where You Can Make Your Mark Rathcoole Veterinary Clinic is a modern, well\-equipped first\-opinion small animal practice, fitted with an in\-house laboratory, digital radiography, dental equipment and ultrasound. The caseload is varied and interesting, offering excellent exposure for developing nurses and plenty of scope for experienced nurses to work confidently and autonomously. The team currently includes a Veterinary Surgeon, a Veterinary Nurse and an ACA, creating a supportive, personal working environment where collaboration and communication come naturally. As the practice continues to grow, this role offers the opportunity to help shape nursing protocols, workflows and the overall direction of the clinic. Who This Role Will Suit We welcome applications from all levels of Veterinary Nurses. Experience is beneficial but not essential, and newly qualified nurses are very welcome to apply. title: Veterinary Practitioner - Small Animal
description: ****Anicare Vets, Blanchardstown, Dublin**** ****Full\-time, Permanent (Part\-time considered)**** ****Salary up to €75,000 DOE**** **A Role Where You Can Truly Thrive** This is an opportunity to join a practice where your clinical skills are valued, your development is supported, and your work\-life balance is genuinely respected. At Anicare Blanchardstown, we’re looking for a small animal veterinary surgeon who wants more than just a job \- someone who enjoys being part of a collaborative team, takes pride in delivering high standards of care, and is keen to continue growing professionally in a well\-equipped, modern environment. Whether you’re looking to deepen your clinical interests, develop a niche, or simply work somewhere you feel supported and trusted, this is a role where you can settle in and thrive. **A Balanced and Thoughtful Working Pattern** This role is structured around an average 40\-hour working week, designed to provide both strong clinical exposure and protected time to recharge. In practice, your weekly rota will typically be built across 4 days, with some weeks closer to 36 hours and others slightly higher, depending on the weekend rota. This balances out across the rota cycle to an average of 40 hours per week. The weekend rota is fair and clearly structured, averaging approximately 1\.5 weekends in every 6 weeks, and is factored into your overall hours. There are no out\-of\-hours or on\-call requirements, and bank holiday commitments are minimal, usually limited to one or two per year. The overall aim is to provide a sustainable, predictable working pattern that supports both clinical quality and a healthy work\-life balance. **A Clinic Designed for Quality Care** Our Blanchardstown practice has recently undergone refurbishment and is equipped to a high standard, allowing you to work cases thoroughly and confidently. You’ll have access to dedicated surgical and dental theatres, digital and dental x\-ray, ultrasound, and a fully equipped in\-house laboratory. We’re particularly proud of our feline\-friendly approach, with separate dog and cat wards and a dedicated cat consult room \- making this an especially appealing environment for anyone with an interest in feline medicine. The caseload is varied, with a healthy mix of medical and surgical work, and excellent scope for someone with a passion for dentistry to really make the most of the facilities available. **A Team That Works Together** You’ll be joining a close\-knit and experienced team of four vets, four nurses, and two support staff. Collaboration is a big part of how we work. There is always someone to discuss cases with, share ideas, or simply support each other through a busy day. Within the team, there is strong clinical expertise, giving you access to valuable support and learning opportunities. As part of the wider Anicare Veterinary Group and IVC network, you’ll also benefit from broader clinical communities, mentorship, and long\-term career pathways. **Who Will Fit In Here** This role would suit a vet with at least a couple of years’ experience in small animal practice who is confident with routine surgery and consultations. It’s not a position suited to a new graduate, as there will be elements of autonomy required. You must be VCI registered and eligible to work in Ireland. We’re looking for someone approachable, team\-oriented, and motivated to continue developing their skills. An interest in feline medicine or dentistry would be a real advantage, but most importantly, you should enjoy good\-quality general practice and working as part of a supportive team. **A Package That Supports You Long\-Term – What We Offer** Alongside a competitive salary, you’ll be supported with a comprehensive benefits package designed around your wellbeing and career progression: **Work Life Balance \& Employee Wellbeing** * 22 days annual leave * Additional holiday entitlement for your birthday * Paid maternity, paternity, and adoptive leave packages * Cycle to Work Scheme * Company Sick Pay \& Bereavement Leave * Access to EAP \& Wellbeing Centre **Career Development** * €1300 annual CPD allowance with 40 hours paid CPD leave * Full funding for any Certificates / Diplomas / PhD’s you may want to pursue * Opportunities for clinical and leadership progression * VCI and VDS memberships fully covered * Dedicated CARE fund \- €1,100 annually to support exceptional cases **Additional Benefits** * Annual Pay Reviews * Employer Pension Contributions * Discounted Veterinary Fees across the IVC Evidensia network * Free Flu Vaccinations * Access to “Pawesome” Benefits – retail and lifestyle discount platform * Opportunity to join company programmes (LGBTQ\+, Wellbeing Champions, Clinical Board and many more) **Let’s Have a Conversation** If you’re looking for a role where you can do your best clinical work, feel part of a genuinely supportive team, and enjoy a balanced lifestyle in Dublin, we’d love to hear from you. Get in touch to find out more \- even if you’re just curious, we’re always happy to have an informal chat. €0 \- €75,000 a year Up to €75,000 DOE We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.
